repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Fixes #39224.
Fixes #39400.

Out of scope for this PR but I think we'll want to update the type annotations to include the possibility of these values being `vim.NIL`.

comment_data.body:
I don't think that treating `nil` as equivalent to `vim.NIL` is correct here. The former is meant to represent `symbol.deprecated = nil` while the latter means that `symbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` property. Those are treated differently in LSP and we should handle them differently here as well. This was why we went through the hassle of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/34849.

`symbol` here is of type `lsp.DocumentSymbol|lsp.SymbolInformation|lsp.WorkspaceSymbol`. The first 2 variants support `deprecated?: boolean`, while `lsp.WorkspaceSymbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` field at all. In the case of `symbol.tags`, the field is either missing or set to an array. The current code would incorrectly accept servers setting `symbol.tags = nil`.

So I would write this as:
```lua
      local is_deprecated = (symbol.deprecated ~= vim.NIL and symbol.deprecated)
        or (symbol.tags ~= vim.NIL and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
        symbol.containerName ~= vim.NIL and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)
+        or (not_nil(symbol.tags) and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
       local text = string.format(
         '[%s] %s%s%s',
         kind,
         symbol.name,
-        symbol.containerName and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',
+        not_nil(symbol.containerName) and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',

comment_data.body:
`not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)` is just checking that it's not `nil`, `vim.NIL`, or `false` (hence the `or nil`).

It already accepted servers setting `symbol.tabs == nil` (and `false`). Are you staying that was a bug?

comment_data.body:
This doesn't work. It needs to check both `nil` and `vim.NIL`. For example, lua_ls, with just `symbol.containerName ~= vim.NIL`:

```

stack traceback:

comment_data.body:
That was `lua-language-server`.

From personally testing the LSPs I have installed, this also applies to:
- `rust-analyzer`
- `json-lsp`
- `fish-lsp`
- `bash-language-server`
- `clangd`

The only language server I have installed that sets `containerName` to `vim.NIL` is `python-lsp-server`.

comment_data.body:
> So I would write this as:
> 
> ```lua
>       local is_deprecated = (symbol.deprecated ~= vim.NIL and symbol.deprecated)
>         or (symbol.tags ~= vim.NIL and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
> ```

Yeah, so this doesn't work either:

```
vim.schedule callback: /home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/shared.lua:336: t: expected table, got nil
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'error'
	/home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/shared.lua:1315: in function 'validate'
	/home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/shared.lua:336: in function 'tbl_contains'
	/home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua:1971: in function 'map_result'

```

comment_data.body:
I see. My main point is:

> I don't think that treating `nil` as equivalent to `vim.NIL` is correct here. The former is meant to represent `symbol.deprecated = nil` while the latter means that `symbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` property. Those are treated differently in LSP and we should handle them differently here as well. This was why we went through the hassle of #34849.

Depending on the possible types of `symbol` here, we should check if it's `nil` or `vim.NIL`. The current code would incorrectly accept invalid server responses.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
> The current code would incorrectly accept invalid server responses.

This functionally just casts `vim.NIL`s to `nil`s for both of these checks, which I believe should be handled the same in this specific case. I understand that #34849 was very important and allowed for logic which distinguishes between the two, but I'm not sure we want to handle these cases any differently here.
comment_data.diff_hunk:

-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
nit/nonblocker: ~~would isnil be more readable?~~

note that this has some overlap with `F.if_nil` (which needs to be renamed...), but that doesn't currently support `vim.NIL`. so perhaps we should make this a similar thing that also supports `vim.NIL`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -129,4 +129,11 @@ function M.term_exitcode()
   return ''
 end
 
+--- Returns false if value is `nil` or `vim.NIL`
+---
+--- @return boolean
+function M.not_nil(value)

comment_data.body:
> The current code would incorrectly accept invalid server responses.

As a compromise, we could at least show a better error message since we've already done the work here.

Otherwise, is there a blocker remaining?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)

       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
> As a compromise, we could at least show a better error message since we've already done the work here.

I'm not sure we can do this. Even if passing `nil` (nothing) is incorrect behavior for servers and they should be passing `vim.NIL` (`null`), many of them send `nil`. A nicer error message would be great, but I think it's probably better to let this behavior slide.

I also haven't looked at the spec so I don't know if this field is optional or not.

> Otherwise, is there a blocker remaining?

Not for me, no.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)

-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
I think it probably does make sense to change this to `isnil`, otherwise we end up with things like `not not_nil()`. Right?

That also aligns with `isarray`, `islist`, and prepares this for adding to public stdlib.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -129,4 +129,11 @@ function M.term_exitcode()
   return ''
 end
 
+--- Returns false if value is `nil` or `vim.NIL`
+---
+--- @return boolean
+function M.not_nil(value)

comment_data.body:
Can do! I liked `not_nil()` because a lot of use cases will involve writing `not isnil()` anyway. But consistency with `isarray` and `islist` is a good idea, and `not not_nil()` is really ugly.

After I make this change, should I open a PR to add this to the stdlib?
comment_data.diff_hunk:

comment_data.body: I'm working on deprecating `vim.F` right now. Are there any functions that you think should be migrated out of the `vim.F` namespace (`vim.F.npcall()` being the most useful looking one, which can be moved to `vim.npcall()`), or should they just all be deprecated with no replacement?

+--- Returns false if value is `nil` or `vim.NIL`

comment_regular_data.body:
> Out of scope for this PR but I think we'll want to update the type annotations to include the possibility of these values being `vim.NIL`.

This is related to the JSON-RPC layer; currently, we have only manually annotated some of the JSON-RPC types. In #38525, I added the complete JSON-RPC types, including this `vim.NIL`.
This fixes 2 bugs, let's unblock it. If there is a followup needed we can do that but I don't see a major risk here.
#39400 it's  not a bug and it shouldn't be treated as one.

oops, I forgot that this also address https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39400 

@superatomic this needs a rebase

Backport failed for `release-0.12`, because it was unable to cherry-pick the commit(s).

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin release-0.12
git worktree add -d .worktree/backport-39225-to-release-0.12 origin/release-0.12
cd .worktree/backport-39225-to-release-0.12
git switch --create backport-39225-to-release-0.12
git cherry-pick -x 1799aaebda4afc0f7c74ff1821374c7bdf4f2c05
```

title: fix(lsp): check for `vim.NIL` where needed
